verses.text: The father warns his son to attend to wisdom, for the lips of the strange woman 'drop as an honeycomb,' her mouth smoother than oil. Temptation speaks sweetly. The flattery that leads to sin is honeyed at the first taste.

The father warns his son to attend to wisdom, for the lips of the strange woman 'drop as an honeycomb,' her mouth smoother than oil. Temptation speaks sweetly. The flattery that leads to sin is honeyed at the first taste.

The father warns his son to attend to wisdom, for the lips of the strange woman 'drop as an honeycomb,' her mouth smoother than oil. Temptation speaks sweetly. The flattery that leads to sin is honeyed at the first taste.

But her end is 'bitter as wormwood, sharp as a twoedged sword'; her feet go down to death, her ways unstable and unknown. The sweetness turns deadly. The path of forbidden pleasure leads, by ways we do not foresee, to ruin.

But her end is 'bitter as wormwood, sharp as a twoedged sword'; her feet go down to death, her ways unstable and unknown. The sweetness turns deadly. The path of forbidden pleasure leads, by ways we do not foresee, to ruin.

But her end is 'bitter as wormwood, sharp as a twoedged sword'; her feet go down to death, her ways unstable and unknown. The sweetness turns deadly. The path of forbidden pleasure leads, by ways we do not foresee, to ruin.

He urges, 'remove thy way far from her,' and come not near her door, lest a man give his honour to others and his years to the cruel. Flee the very approach of sin. Distance, not nearness, is the safety of the tempted.

Else strangers feast on his wealth, and he mourns at the last when flesh and body are consumed, confessing, 'How have I hated instruction!' Regret comes too late. The pleasures of sin leave only ruin and bitter self-reproach.

Instead, 'drink waters out of thine own cistern,' rejoicing in 'the wife of thy youth,' her love satisfying always. Faithful marriage is God's good gift. Joy and contentment are found at home, not in the stranger's house.

Why be ravished by a strange woman, when 'the ways of man are before the eyes of the LORD,' and his own sins will hold him fast? Nothing is hidden from God. The sinner is bound at last by the very cords of his own iniquity.
